title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Hormonal Imbalance
intro: Herbal teas for Hormonal imbalance are a type of natural remedy that involves the consumption of tea made from various herbs, plants, and flowers to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. These teas can treat hormonal imbalance due to their ability to regulate hormonal production, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat hormonal imbalance include peppermint, which helps to regulate menstrual cycles, and ginger, which aids in reducing stress and inflammation. Other beneficial herbal teas include chamomile, which promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, red clover, which helps to regulate estrogen levels, and dandelion root, which supports liver function and hormone regulation.

preparations.herb_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa teas, also known as black cohosh, have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments such as menopausal symptoms, including hot flashes and mood swings. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat this ailment include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to regulate hormonal balance and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of black cohosh, including triterpene glycosides and phenolic acids, have been shown to bind to estrogen receptors, mimicking the effects of estrogen and alleviating menopausal symptoms. The benefits of black cohosh tea include reduced frequency and severity of hot flashes, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall quality of life for women experiencing menopausal symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Cimicifuga racemosa root.
Use 1-2 tablespoons of the root for every cup of water.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Cimicifuga racemosa root to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day to help balance hormones.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of Sexual Medicine", Cimicifuga racemosa teas for hormonal imbalance may be recommended for several female sexual dysfunction complaints, particularly in menopausal women, but more clinical studies are needed to establish its efficacy and safety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cimicifuga racemosa teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to the high concentration of active compounds like sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. To minimize these risks, it is essential to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea in large quantities, especially when combined with other herbal remedies or medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Cimicifuga racemosa tea
Start with 1/2 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, for 7-10 days
Monitor your body's response and adjust the dosage as needed
Increase the dosage to 1 cup of tea, 2-3 times a day, if needed
Continue for 3-6 months to achieve optimal hormonal balance

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments due to their adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat hormonal imbalance by reducing stress, promoting the balance of sex hormones, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of stress hormones like cortisol and improving insulin sensitivity. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide numerous benefits in managing hormonal imbalance, including reduced menstrual irregularities, improved fertility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help balance hormonal levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "JBRA assisted reproduction", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for hormonal imbalance may help in improving the adverse effects of diabetes caused by PCOS by lowering lipid and blood glucose levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance, but possible side effects may include high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and edema due to its aldosterone-like effects. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is essential to consume it in moderation, ideally under 400mg per day, and to monitor blood pressure and potassium levels regularly, as prolonged or excessive use can exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Take 200-300mg of Glycyrrhizin per serving (1 cup).
Consume for 1-3 months, then reassess hormonal levels.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosing.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been used for centuries to treat hormonal imbalance ailments due to their adaptogenic and calming properties. They help to treat hormonal imbalance by regulating the body's response to stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, thereby reducing the symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, including avenacoside, avenalol, and avenanthramides, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to their therapeutic effects. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas can provide relief from hormonal imbalance symptoms, improve mood, and enhance overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa oat straw, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Rinse the tea infuser with boiling water, then add 2 tablespoons of Avena sativa oat straw.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the oat straw in the tea infuser.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day to help balance hormones.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, which can exacerbate conditions such as hypoglycemia and thyroid disorders. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it's essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage, avoid consuming it with other sedatives or stimulants, and be cautious when taking it with medications like blood thinners or antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 teaspoon of Avena sativa powder in a cup of warm water.
Drink 1 cup of Avena sativa tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of powder per cup for 3-7 days.
Gradually increase dosage to 1-2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Use Avena sativa tea for 2-6 weeks to help balance hormones naturally.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, particularly in women, due to their unique properties that help regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms such as mood swings and breast tenderness. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including iridoids like aucubin and agnuside, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to balance the hormonal system. By interacting with the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, Vitex agnus-castus teas help to regulate the production of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH), which in turn influences the secretion of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH). The benefits of using Vitex agnus-castus teas to treat hormonal imbalance ailments include improved menstrual regularity, reduced symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Vitex agnus-castus berries or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh Vitex leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried or fresh Vitex.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and transfer the tea to a cup. Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally in the morning and before bedtime, to help balance hormonal levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMJ (Clinical research ed.)", Vitex agnus-castus teas for hormonal imbalance, specifically in the relief of premenstrual syndrome symptoms, have been shown to be effective in reducing symptoms and improving the condition, with a 52% responder rate compared to 24% for placebo.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can help alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, but some individuals may experience side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its potential to interact with certain medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions. When using Vitex agnus-castus teas to treat hormonal imbalance, it is essential to be cautious and take precautions, including avoiding its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being aware of potential interactions with medications such as blood thinners and certain antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Vitex agnus-castus tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons after 2 weeks if needed.
Take tea for 6-12 months to notice significant hormonal balance.
Consult a healthcare professional before taking for 3 months or more.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments due to their calming and balancing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat hormonal imbalance by reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to possess estrogen-regulating properties, which can help to balance hormonal levels. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia teas may also help to improve sleep quality and reduce symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as mood swings and hot flashes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers.
Bring 1 cup of water to boil in a pot.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep the flowers for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea strainer.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help balance hormonal levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Fitoterapia", Lavandula angustifolia teas for hormonal imbalance may be a promising candidate for treating polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) by increasing progesterone levels and regulating menstrual cycles, similar to metformin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be beneficial in treating hormonal imbalance ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat hormonal imbalance, it is essential to be cautious and aware that prolonged consumption may lead to liver damage, and it is also recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons after 3-5 days.
Use 1-2 cups per day for 2-4 weeks to notice improvements.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments due to their properties, including adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which help to stabilize hormone levels and reduce oxidative stress. preparations.preparation_study: According to Proceedings of the Society for Experimental Biology and Medicine, Curcuma longa teas were found to be one of the six highest ER-binding herbs that are commonly consumed, with the ability to compete with estradiol and bind to intracellular receptors for progesterone (PR) and estradiol (ER) in intact human breast cancer cells.

Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including starting with small doses and gradually increasing as needed, and being mindful of potential interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, particularly those related to the thyroid and adrenal glands. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to regulate hormonal imbalances by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents, including silymarin and silibinin, in Silybum marianum teas have been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormone production and metabolism, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat hormonal imbalance ailments include improved thyroid function, reduced cortisol levels, and enhanced overall hormone regulation, leading to a reduction in symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and mood swings.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and leaves.
Place the flowers and leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the Silybum marianum.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, after consulting a doctor.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Silybum marianum teas, which contain the compound silymarin, may have a key role in treating hormonal imbalance in women with PCOS by affecting hormone levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, but possible side effects may include digestive issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. To use Silybum marianum teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount as needed, and to avoid consumption if you have any pre-existing kidney or liver conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Dioscorea villosa teas have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, such as polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and menopausal symptoms, due to their ability to regulate estrogen and progesterone levels. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea villosa teas, including diosgenin and other saponins, help to modulate hormonal activity and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. Dioscorea villosa teas may help to treat hormonal imbalance ailments by reducing inflammation, improving insulin sensitivity, and promoting a healthy balance of hormones in the body. The benefits of using Dioscorea villosa teas to treat hormonal imbalance ailments include reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression, improved menstrual regularity, and a decrease in the risk of developing conditions such as endometriosis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Dioscorea villosa root and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Boil the water and then add the Dioscorea villosa root. Reduce heat to low.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the root and let the tea cool. Store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
To make the tea, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the cooled tea with 1 cup of hot water. Drink 1-2 cups a day for hormonal balance.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Dioscorea villosa teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Dioscorea villosa teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it for extended periods of time to minimize the risk of interactions with other medications and hormonal imbalances.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, including menopausal symptoms and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), due to their adaptogenic and hormone-regulating properties. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including lignans, schisantherin A, and schisandrins, help to modulate the body's stress response and regulate hormone production, thereby alleviating symptoms such as hot flashes, mood swings, and irregular menstrual cycles. By stimulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, Schisandra chinensis teas promote a balance between estrogen and progesterone levels, which is essential for maintaining optimal reproductive health. The benefits of Schisandra chinensis teas in treating hormonal imbalance ailments include reduced symptoms, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being, making them a valuable natural remedy for women experiencing hormonal imbalances.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Schisandra chinensis berries and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Schisandra chinensis berries to a tea infuser or directly to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the berries and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the berries. You can add honey or sugar if you prefer it sweet.
Drink 1-2 cups of Schisandra chinensis tea per day, ideally 30 minutes after meals, to help balance hormones.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be effective in treating hormonal imbalance, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications and to monitor blood sugar levels, as Schisandra chinensis may affect blood sugar control and exacerbate conditions such as diabetes.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas, also known as maypop or passionflower tea, have been used for centuries to treat various ailments, including hormonal imbalance. The tea's properties, such as its calming and sedative effects, help to alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, have been shown to interact with neurotransmitters and hormone receptors, regulating the body's hormonal balance. By consuming Passiflora incarnata tea, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced stress and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and a more balanced hormonal system, ultimately alleviating symptoms of hormonal imbalances.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers.
Use a tablespoon to measure 1-2 tablespoons of the dried leaves and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Steep the measured leaves and flowers in the boiled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage hormonal imbalance.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Electronic physician, Passiflora incarnata teas for hormonal imbalance have been shown to be effective in treating acute menopausal syndrome, including hot flashes, through different mechanisms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include starting with low doses and gradually increasing as needed, avoiding use with other sedatives or medications, and being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas have been traditionally used to treat hormonal imbalance ailments due to their adaptogenic and hormone-regulating properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by modulating the body's response to stress, promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, and regulating the production of hormones such as insulin and thyroid hormones. Bioactive constituents like flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides in Astragalus membranaceus teas contribute to its therapeutic effects by exerting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory actions. The benefits of this herbal preparation include improved hormonal balance, enhanced immune function, and reduced symptoms of hormonal imbalance ailments such as menopausal hot flashes and mood disturbances.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Pour the boiling water over the Astragalus membranaceus root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser or strainer.
Drink the tea as is, or add honey to taste, 2-3 times a day to help balance hormonal levels.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Astragalus membranaceus teas for hormonal imbalance may have a moderate level of estrogenic activity, with a relative potency (RP) of 8.47 x 10(-5), indicating potential efficacy in alleviating menopausal symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Astragalus membranaceus teas can be used to treat hormonal imbalance, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Astragalus membranaceus teas, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it with other medications that may interact, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications.

preparation_study: According to Menopause (New York, N.Y.), Cimicifuga racemosa teas for hormonal imbalance showed no statistically significant effectiveness in alleviating vasomotor symptoms and improving quality of life in a 16-week randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for hormonal imbalance may be beneficial in alleviating menopausal symptoms, specifically hot flashes, as some botanicals and dietary supplements, such as black cohosh and soy products, have been found to be effective in reducing these symptoms.
